title: Blakea toachiensis
intro: Blakea toachiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, native to Ecuador. The plant has been traditionally used in Ecuadorian medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. Blakea toachiensis contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Blakea toachiensis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it may also be used in combination with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Blakea toachiensis is classified within the family Calophyllaceae. This classification places it within the order Malpighiales. The Calophyllaceae family is further grouped into the superorder Rosanae, which includes several other flowering plant families. Blakea toachiensis is also classified as a member of the genus Blakea.
